channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling
item.description:
The Law Centre today delivered a formal letter to Dr. Jerome Larkin, Director of the Rhode Island Department of Health (RIDOH), demanding that the agency immediately cease enforcement of Rhode Island’s ban on conversion therapy for minors. The action follows the U.S. Supreme Court’s March 21, 2026 decision in Chiles v. Salazar, which struck down Colorado’s similar law as a violation of the First Amendment.

The post Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] Minnesota Parents, We’re About to Lose Out on $800 Million for Our Children
item.description:
By Zach Freimark and Sarah Hitchcock America First Policy Institute On July 4, 2025, Congress passed, on a bipartisan vote, a package of reconciliations, commonly referred to as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. Included in the bill was a provision that created the Education Freedom Tax Credit (EFTC), which funds elementary and secondary education […]

The post Minnesota Parents, We’re About to Lose Out on $800 Million for Our Children appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Sine Die
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Sine Die Lawmakers returned to Jackson expecting veto overrides, but what followed was one of the more unusual endings to a legislative session in recent memory. We unpack the final day’s twists, the political fallout, and close the book on the 2026 session.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Sine Die first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] “Tax Me More” Fund Not Seeing Much Action Despite Tax Hikers’ False Appeals to Patriotism
item.description:
Americans are allowed to voluntarily contribute money to the U.S. Treasury’s “tax me more” fund. But not much money is being donated despite the false appeals to “patriotism” peddled by tax hikers. In calling for tax increases, affluent Democrats and progressive activists try to assert that Americans opposed to such tax hikes are lacking in […]

The post “Tax Me More” Fund Not Seeing Much Action Despite Tax Hikers’ False Appeals to Patriotism appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Secretary of State Bellows Butchers Another Ballot Question
item.description:
In the fall, Maine voters will have the opportunity to vote on a citizen referendum aimed at protecting girls’ sports by requiring schools to separate athletic teams and certain facilities based on biological sex as recorded at birth. Before they ever get the chance to vote on the policy, however, there is a critically important […]

The post Secretary of State Bellows Butchers Another Ballot Question appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas Has a Cost Problem—And It’s Driving People Away
item.description: Kansas isn’t collapsing. That would at least get attention. What’s happening is quieter and more dangerous: the state is becoming too expensive, too fragmented, and too average to compete, while other states pull ahead. The new 2026 Kansas Green Book lays it out clearly. Kansas is not leading where it matters. It is lagging, and […]

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] Why Walz’s proposed social media tax is a bad idea
item.description:
It would run afoul of the First Amendment, among other issues. By Annette Meeks Guest contributor to the Minnesota Star Tribune April 20, 2026 ••• What do progressives do when they run out of other people’s money to spend? You don’t have to look any further than Minnesota to find out: Recently Gov. Tim Walz […]

The post Why Walz’s proposed social media tax is a bad idea appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Strengthening Her Family, Building Her Community: Shaneika Murphy’s Passion for Helping Others Flourish
item.description:
After completing the Raising Highly Capable Kids workshop as a parent, Shaneika Murphy now supports other parents as a program facilitator. Her sons volunteer with the program, too. Together, the Murphys are a shining example of how strong families build strong communities.

The post Strengthening Her Family, Building Her Community: Shaneika Murphy’s Passion for Helping Others Flourish appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Raising Highly Capable Kids Program Increases Positive Values and Social Skills in Children
item.description:
GCO and its partners offered 15 RHCK workshops at 12 locations in 2025. Participants took pre- and post-workshop surveys that illustrated the ways families had grown stronger throughout the program.

The post Raising Highly Capable Kids Program Increases Positive Values and Social Skills in Children appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Empowerms] Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service
item.description:
Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service Mississippi Secretary of State Michael Watson returns to the Empower Podcast as he launches his campaign for Lt. Governor. In this conversation, Grant sits down with Watson for a more personal look at the experiences and convictions that have shaped his leadership. From growing up on the Gulf…

The post Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] The NJEA Is Putting Politics Before Kids, Again
item.description:
New Jersey should opt into the Education Freedom Tax Credit. The union’s opposition tells you everything about whose interests it […]

The post The NJEA Is Putting Politics Before Kids, Again first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: ALEC President Jonathan Williams on Nebraska’s Latest Ranking
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Jonathan Williams, co-author of Rich States, Poor States and President and Chief Economist at the American Legislative Exchange Council. During the episode Jonathan and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss the latest rankings in new edition of Rich States, Poor States, how Nebraska can improve,...

The post Nebraskanomics: ALEC President Jonathan Williams on Nebraska’s Latest Ranking appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Sales Tax Modernization Could Help Nebraska Stay Competitive
item.description:
In an increasingly competitive environment, states are competing every day for workers, entrepreneurs, investment, and new residents. Tax policy plays a major role in those decisions. States with more competitive tax structures are often better positioned to attract business investment, encourage job creation, and retain families looking for opportunity.  For Nebraska, that matters. Neighboring states...

The post Sales Tax Modernization Could Help Nebraska Stay Competitive appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF Petitions US Supreme Court to Protect Free Speech
item.description:
Thanks to our friends at the Liberty Justice Center the Rio Grande Foundation is appealing for certiorari (an appeal for our case to be heard) against New Mexico Secretary of State Maggie Toulouse Oliver. The case is called: Rio Grande Foundation v. Oliver. The case is based on the proposition that Americans have a constitutional […]

The post RGF Petitions US Supreme Court to Protect Free Speech appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] 2026 Legislative Wrap-Up
item.description:
As the 132nd Maine Legislature raced toward adjournment, lawmakers pushed through a wave of major bills in just a matter of days. The final stretch of the session was fast-moving, and decisions were made that will shape Maine’s economy, healthcare system, and tax climate for years to come. Maine Policy Institute closely tracked many of […]

The post 2026 Legislative Wrap-Up appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF in Las Cruces Sun News: Las Cruces overspent. It’s time to cut | Opinion
item.description:
The following appeared in the Las Cruces Sun-News on April 26, 2026. After years of overspending the City of Las Cruces, like Albuquerque, faces a budget deficit.  Unlike Albuquerque which has lost people, Las Cruces continues to grow and has seen a 7.08% increase since the 2020 census. That means that the City can and should be able to […]

The post RGF in Las Cruces Sun News: Las Cruces overspent. It’s time to cut | Opinion appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Saving Money Should Not Be a Thankless Task for Legislators
item.description: Not too long ago, I had a conversation with a staff member of one of Idaho’s US Senators. The conversation centered around why it was so difficult to get federal spending under control - which clearly has not happened. This staffer remarked to me that when Congress cuts spending, they hear loudly from those who […]

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Unserving ‘the Unhoused’ OUR MAYOR has thrown down the NIMBY gauntlet. She is trying to shame opponents of her plan to build a $3.8 million “homeless services” center downtown, a couple of blocks from the  art museum. She says they selfishly don’t want the center in their backyard but cannot offer an alternative plan. I am Read the full article...

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] Coalition forms to educate Arkansans on dangers of Educational Rights Amendment
[Opportunityarkansas] Two groups leading initiatives to end Arkansas’ income tax
[Mspolicy] Investing in the Next Generation of Mississippi Leaders
item.description:
In April, we had the opportunity to welcome students from Pearl River County High School’s Student Council to our office during their visit to Jackson. As part of a broader trip to tour the State Capitol and meet with legislators, the group made time to sit down with Douglas Carswell and our team to learn […]

The post Investing in the Next Generation of Mississippi Leaders appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Fuel Prices Need Abundance
item.description: Kansas families need lower fuel costs through more supply, less inflation, and fewer government-made costs. Fuel prices hit families fast. A gallon of gas is not just a number on a sign. It is the cost of getting to work, taking kids to school, delivering goods, that long-planned summer road trip, and keeping rural Kansas […]

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Kent, Kefalas tout defeat of tax cuts as major defensive win this session
item.description:
Joe Kent, executive president of the Grassroot Institute of Hawaii, and Ted Kefalas, Grassroot’s director of strategic campaigns, joined Rick Hamada on KHVH radio May 4 to discuss the state Legislature’s final days and reflect on a challenging session dominated by defensive battles against tax increases and budget concerns. Kent characterized this year’s legislative session […]

The post Kent, Kefalas tout defeat of tax cuts as major defensive win this session appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Untangling occupational licensing rules
item.description:
This year’s legislative session is winding down, and it has certainly had its highs and lows. One positive result I’d like to tell you about is a bill on the verge of final approval that would actually move regulations on local entrepreneurs in the right direction. SB2876 is a relatively noncontroversial bill that proposes replacing burdensome […]

The post Untangling occupational licensing rules appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Two labor law updates for the 21st century
item.description:
Remote work, flex time and independent contracting have reshaped the American workforce, creating new opportunities for both employers and employees. But in some states, including New Hampshire, outdated labor laws create obstacles to innovation, lost opportunities for employees and unnecessary costs for employers.  To modernize New Hampshire labor laws, legislators are addressing two outdated statutory […]

The post Two labor law updates for the 21st century appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Can Oil Industry Lawsuits Compel Rational Energy Policy?
item.description:
When asked in a recent interview why California has the highest gasoline prices in the nation, Jodie Muller, the President of the Western States Petroleum Association, began by stating the following: “You can’t point a finger at one particular person, because, unfortunately, it is decades of policies layered on top of one another. You have local air...

The post Can Oil Industry Lawsuits Compel Rational Energy Policy? first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Wisconsin Gubernatorial Candidates Share Their Vision for the State
item.description:
Wisconsin Gubernatorial Candidates Share Their Vision for the State What’s Next for Wisconsin? Candidates for Governor Weigh In As Wisconsin prepares for its next gubernatorial election, voters deserve a clear understanding of where candidates stand on the issues that matter most. That’s why our organization sent a detailed questionnaire to every declared candidate for governor […]

The post Wisconsin Gubernatorial Candidates Share Their Vision for the State appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: James Briggs: The New Journalism WE DON’T ENJOY discouraging a fellow journalist. First of all, nobody cares. It is “inside baseball,” as an editor of mine liked to say. Our members have more important things to do. Still, any job has standards and after some decades in the business (note that I didn’t say “profession”) I have a Read the full article...

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Report: Several Wausau Schools Among Wisconsin’s Very Best
item.description:
For Immediate Release:  May 7, 2026 Contact: Press@ReformingGoverment.org   IRG Report: Several Wausau Schools Among Wisconsin’s Very Best Jefferson Elementary, Newman Catholic recognized for student success   Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) released on May 7 “Wausau Wins: How Wausau Became a Wisconsin Education Leader,” a report highlighting the city’s standout […]

The post IRG Report: Several Wausau Schools Among Wisconsin’s Very Best appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Beacontn] Beacon Poll: May 2026 Results
item.description: Today, we released the latest installment of the Beacon Poll, a statewide survey of 1,200 Tennessee voters featuring questions about voters’ views on energy sources and production in Tennessee, the condition of roads and what revenue sources could be used to improve them, and political approval ratings at the state and federal level.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Reflections on the May Day demonstrations from a native Chicagoan
item.description:
Growing up in Chicago, May Day was always something discussed in my history classes but was never taught as a positive event, or something to be repeated. 

The post Reflections on the May Day demonstrations from a native Chicagoan appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Behind the curtain, Evers administration diverts taxpayer money to fund environmental bureaucracies
item.description:
The Wisconsin Department of Administration has spent nearly $2 million operating two state offices that have since 2019 been denied funding by the Legislature.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Cascade Questions Evidence Behind Metro’s War on Cars
item.description:
Cascade President John A. Charles, Jr. Submits Testimony to Metro Council on its...

The post Cascade Questions Evidence Behind Metro’s War on Cars appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Taxpayers Come First as Iowa Wraps Up 2026 Legislative Session
item.description:
Iowa lawmakers advanced major taxpayer-focused reforms during the 2026 session, including a 2% cap on many local property tax levies, school funding reforms, a larger Homestead Exemption, and other changes projected to save taxpayers more than $4 billion over six years. The legislature also prioritized conservative budgeting by limiting state spending growth to 1.4% and advanced a constitutional amendment requiring a two-thirds legislative vote to increase the income tax, giving voters the opportunity to strengthen long-term taxpayer protections. Lawmakers approved additional oversight of federal funds by requiring regular reporting on large federal grants, reflecting growing concerns about national debt, federal instability, and the long-term consequences attached to federal spending.

The post Taxpayers Come First as Iowa Wraps Up 2026 Legislative Session appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] STATEMENT FROM JERSEY1ST, NEW JERSEY POLICY INSTITUTE, AND AMERICANS FOR PROSPERITY–NEW JERSEY ON NEW WORKER CLASSIFICATION RULE

item.title: [Mspolicy] The Recovery of the American Mind: Why Suicidal Empathy Is the Next Conservative Frontier
item.description:
It was a particular pleasure this week to welcome Gad Saad and his wife to Mississippi. Gad is one of the most important conservative thinkers writing today. His new book, Suicidal Empathy: Dying to Be Kind, is published next month, and I am the proud owner of a signed copy. Gad Saad's new book is […]

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Teacher Appreciation Should Not Be Tied to Spending on Education
item.description: This week is Teacher Appreciation Week across the country, and it prompted Governor Brad Little to issue a press release where he had this to say: “This is Teacher Appreciation Week, a meaningful opportunity to recognize the people who stand at the front of our classrooms each day, shaping the future of our state. I […]

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Warns the WIAA to End Illegal ﻿Race- and Sex-Based Quotas
item.description:
WILL warned the Wisconsin Interscholastic Athletic Association (WIAA) to scrap its illegal race and sex-based quotas. The WIAA, which governs sports for nearly 90,000 Wisconsin students, denies individuals the ability to seek certain WIAA leadership positions based solely on their race or sex.

The post WILL Warns the WIAA to End Illegal ﻿Race- and Sex-Based Quotas appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Arizona teachers union actively covering up sexual misconduct probe
item.description:
When allegations of sexual misconduct involving students surface in a public school, the response should be immediate and obvious — protect students, uncover the truth and hold accountable anyone who failed to act.

The post Arizona teachers union actively covering up sexual misconduct probe appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] The Big, Beautiful Bill is law. Now Arkansas has to get it right.
item.description:
Passed almost one year ago, the famous “One Big Beautiful Bill” contains the most transformational welfare reforms in modern history. […]

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] WAOW: Wausau Schools recognized during Teachers Appreciation Week
item.description:
Wausau Schools recognized during Teachers Appreciation Week WAUSAU, Wis. (WAOW) – This week is Teacher Appreciation week, and the non-profit group Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) is applauding Wausau schools. IRG is a non-profit dedicated to making the government better, ensuring kids have good schools to attend. They awarded Newman Catholic, Thomas Jefferson Elementary, and […]

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Historic 2024 tax cuts rescued for majority of Hawaii residents
item.description:
It was only two years ago that, to much fanfare and celebration, our lawmakers boldly passed history-making income tax cuts to provide struggling Hawaii families with much-needed relief from the state’s crippling cost of living. Those sweeping cuts were scheduled to phase in through 2031, which seems to have given state officials time to reconsider […]

The post Historic 2024 tax cuts rescued for majority of Hawaii residents appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.description:

In this episode of The Empower Podcast, Grant Callen sits down with urban strategist and entrepreneur Josh McManus to discuss the rise, decline, and revitalization of American cities, and why he believes Jackson still has enormous potential. Drawing…

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Dictionary Policing: What Omaha’s “Censored Shop” Tells Us About State Cartels
item.description:
If you’re in Omaha’s Blackstone District and you’re looking for a good cocktail, you might stumble into a tucked-away speakeasy now called the “Censored Shop.” Until recently, it went by “Barber Shop Blackstone.” According to the Nebraska Examiner, that name wasn’t a trick—it was a nod to the owners’ dad, who spent his life as...

The post Dictionary Policing: What Omaha’s “Censored Shop” Tells Us About State Cartels appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Artificial Intelligence in Our State
item.description:
Louisiana’s innovators are using artificial intelligence (AI) in groundbreaking ways to improve the lives and livelihoods of our state. Celebrating this progress around Louisiana is particularly valuable as proposed state and federal regulations multiply. The Pelican Institute’s Center for Technology and Innovation has collected and is continuing to update a list of news stories that...

The post Artificial Intelligence in Our State appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Will Law] KOSCHNICK v. Evers
item.description:
WILL has filed a federal lawsuit on behalf of Terri Koschnick and Joy Buchman, Wisconsin-licensed counselors, challenging a Wisconsin rule banning consensual, client-driven Christian counseling. The challenge follows an 8-1 Supreme Court ruling that Colorado's identical law was unconstitutional "viewpoint discrimination," the "most blatant" kind of First Amendment violation.

The post KOSCHNICK v. Evers appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Will Law] Counselors Sue Evers Admin Over Christian Counseling Ban
item.description:
WILL has filed a federal lawsuit on behalf of Terri Koschnick and Joy Buchman, Wisconsin-licensed counselors, challenging a Wisconsin rule banning consensual, client-driven Christian counseling. The challenge follows an 8-1 Supreme Court ruling that Colorado’s identical law was unconstitutional “viewpoint discrimination,” the “most blatant” kind of First Amendment violation.

The post Counselors Sue Evers Admin Over Christian Counseling Ban appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] When it Comes to Water, California Needs to Think Big Again
item.description:
For most of the previous century, Californians successfully designed and built big water infrastructure. In sixty years, from 1910 through 1970, we built the most impressive system of interbasin transfers in the world. The Los Angeles Aqueduct, Hetch Hetchy Aqueduct, Colorado River Aqueduct, Delta Mendota Canal, Friant-Kern Canal, and California Aqueduct. Altogether these conveyances are...

The post When it Comes to Water, California Needs to Think Big Again first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] No Tough Questions Allowed for the California Gubernatorial Front Runner
item.description:
This week, Tim and Matt discuss the latest news in the governor’s race – including a very embarrassing interview by nominal front runner Xavier Becerra, and Katie Porter supporters charging their candidate is receiving sexist treatment over the coverage of her “likeability”. They also talk about the big Los Angeles mayoral debate, and preview Gov. […]

The post No Tough Questions Allowed for the California Gubernatorial Front Runner appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Mackinac] Virtue-signaling with state holidays
item.description: A Michigan tradition since the 19th century

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Higher pay, fewer trips: What Seattle’s gig law got wrong
item.description:
According to researchers at Carnegie Mellon University analyzing Seattle’s law in a National Bureau of Economic Research study, the average base pay per delivery jumped from about $5.37 to $12.52, but tips fell so much that more than one-third of that gain disappeared, and monthly earnings for highly active drivers were virtually unchanged after the first […]

The post Higher pay, fewer trips: What Seattle’s gig law got wrong appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Iowa Embraces Restraint in New Budget
item.description:
Governor Kim Reynolds and the legislature limited Fiscal Year 2027 spending growth to 1.4% in response to slower revenue growth, economic uncertainty, and the continued phase-in of income tax cuts that allow Iowans to keep more of their own money. Public education and Medicaid continue to dominate Iowa’s budget and are growing faster than overall revenue, raising concerns that those programs could crowd out other priorities without long-term reforms. Iowa’s strong reserves and Taxpayer Relief Fund were made possible through conservative budgeting, and continued spending restraint will be necessary to protect those reserves and preserve the ability to deliver future income tax relief.

The post Iowa Embraces Restraint in New Budget appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] A new era for civics education in Idaho
item.description: Idaho took a significant step this year toward strengthening civics education and preparing the next generation for informed citizenship.

item.description:
A federal judge found Cook County liable for damages to taxpayers who lost the equity in their homes because of an unconstitutional state law.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Billionaires Are Not The Problem
item.description:
“You can’t earn a billion dollars. You just can’t earn that. You can get market power. You can break rules. You can do all sorts of things. You can abuse labor laws. You can pay people less than what they’re worth. But you can’t earn that.” These comments made by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez on a […]

The post Billionaires Are Not The Problem appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Why the Paramount-Skydance deal is good for California
item.description:
By now, almost every Californian with eyeballs and a memory of the recent past can tell you the story of the failed High-Speed Rail – how voters in 2008 approved a roughly $33 billion project to carry passengers from Los Angeles to San Francisco in lightning speed beginning in 2020. We’re six years past that...

The post Why the Paramount-Skydance deal is good for California first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Play for the Team on Your Jersey: It’s an Integrity Thing.
item.description: Imagine your favorite sports team. They all wear the same uniform, stand under the same banner, and operate under the same creed — not just to play, but to win according to a shared playbook. Each player brings unique strengths, but when they join the team, they all commit to the same rules, values, and […]

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Big federal bucks so far produce a paltry 21 EV charging stations in Wisconsin
item.description:
Millions of dollars have been spent on the aim to build 80 charging stations for electric vehicles at gas stations, hotels, and other venues across Wisconsin.

The post Big federal bucks so far produce a paltry 21 EV charging stations in Wisconsin appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Failure of tax-and-schools deal offers chance to do better
item.description:
Think of the failure of the $1.8 billion tax-and-spending deal between Gov. Tony Evers and the Legislature as a second chance at better policy.

The post Failure of tax-and-schools deal offers chance to do better appeared first on Badger Institute.

By Naomi Inman It’s election time in Oregon and the Oregonian editorial board...

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Chicago population up for third straight year, but growth slows
item.description:
Chicago grew for a third straight year, but the growth is slowing down compared to previous years.

The post Chicago population up for third straight year, but growth slows appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Blog: Massachusetts Needs Growth—Not More of the Same
item.description:
Last week, the Massachusetts Taxpayers Foundation (MTF) released a “Bulletin” announcing its opposition to the tax cut ballot initiative. Odd for a taxpayers foundation to oppose a reduction in taxes. Even odder, however, was the Bulletin’s lack of concern about the Commonwealth’s current economic climate: dismal business formation, job losses, and weakening economic growth across the state. 

The post Blog: Massachusetts Needs Growth—Not More of the Same appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Johnlocke] AI demand helps push Microsoft away from climate goals
item.description:
Kevin Killough writes for Just the News about an interesting shift in Microsoft’s priorities. During the Biden era, Microsoft was a leader in Big Tech’s push to eliminate fossil fuels and power the world with energy from wind and solar farms.  In January 2020, the company set ambitious goals to become carbon negative within a...

The post AI demand helps push Microsoft away from climate goals appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] ‘Affordable housing: No matter what they say in public, they're terrified of it’
item.description: Alex Cartwright on the challenge of converting hotel space into housing

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Finding the missing middle: How to build more starter homes
item.description:
“Affordable housing” has become a commonly used phrase in California because there is so little of it. Activists demand it and policymakers promise they can produce lots if it through their clever legislating. But their plans usually include housing where they want it (near public transit centers), not necessarily where buyers want it, and built […]

The post Finding the missing middle: How to build more starter homes appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Counties urge Pritzker to say yes to donor funds for students
item.description:
Twelve county boards to date have adopted resolutions pushing for the governor to opt into a donor-funded federal program for academic needs.

The post Counties urge Pritzker to say yes to donor funds for students appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Horse Thieves Matter THE SON OF PIONEERS in the Flint Hills of Kansas, my sheriff grandfather was familiar with the story of “the Sedgwick County Anti-Horse-Thieving Association” — or the SCAHTA as it was never known because they were blessedly free of acronyms back then.  Let us just say that it would not have fit into the defund-the-police messaging, Read the full article...

item.title: [Americanexperiment] New York State weakens climate law
item.description:
Say it ain’t so! From Bloomberg, New York Prepares to Rollback Ambitious Climate Laws Governor Kathy Hochul cited soaring energy prices as a reason. I had honestly thought that the soaring energy prices were the goal, not a drawback. Bloomberg reports, All over the world, the energy crisis is making it harder for governments to...

The post New York State weakens climate law appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Terror Support, Foreign Extremists and Homegrown Abuse: TexAM’s Radical Leaders Betray the Institution’s Underpinning Agenda
item.description:
At the direction of Texas Gov. Greg Abbott, the Texas Higher Education Coordinating Board has ordered the Texas American Muslim University at Dallas (TexAM) to halt its operations, stating that the organization is not a recognized university and thus “prohibited by law from granting or offering to grant degrees.” But there are other reasons for...

The post Terror Support, Foreign Extremists and Homegrown Abuse: TexAM’s Radical Leaders Betray the Institution’s Underpinning Agenda first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Governor Ron DeSantis Unveils Statue Honoring “Father of the Constitution” James Madison in Florida’s Madison County
item.description:
MADISON, Fla.—Today, Governor Ron DeSantis unveiled a new statue honoring President James Madison as part of Florida’s continued...

item.description:
The Coalition for Safe and Reliable Energy petitioned the New York State Public Service Commission to hold a hearing to....

The post It is Time to Rethink New York’s Renewable Energy Mandates appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Wyoming Supreme Court jumpstarts education choice funding
item.description: Education choice in Wyoming and throughout the United States has the potential to usher in an era of learning focused on student success, tailored to their individual needs. This decision is a great start towards that goal.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] St. Louis Judge Rejects Police Effort to Withhold Public Records
item.description:
Sunshine laws exist for a reason—to ensure governments aren’t operating in the shadows and to hold leaders accountable by allowing..

The post St. Louis Judge Rejects Police Effort to Withhold Public Records appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Great Nations Aren't Destroyed by Enemies. They're Destroyed by Debt.
item.description:
Imagine your family spent more this year than you earned. It wouldn't be comfortable, but you'd manage. Now imagine your family had spent more than it earned every single year since 2001. By now you'd be destitute. Well, that is exactly what the federal government has been doing. For the past 25 years — ever […]

The post Great Nations Aren't Destroyed by Enemies. They're Destroyed by Debt. appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] U.S. Energy Secretary Calls for More Capitalism in American Electricity Production — Is Louisiana Listening?
item.description: The post U.S. Energy Secretary Calls for More Capitalism in American Electricity Production — Is Louisiana Listening? appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] If not tax credits, what should legislators do about childcare?
item.description:
Something must be done about the high cost of childcare in Minnesota. But as I have illustrated in a previous article, solutions like tax credits fail to address why costs are high in the first place. So, what can lawmakers do about the lack of affordable childcare in Minnesota? American Experiment’s newly released policy briefing...

The post If not tax credits, what should legislators do about childcare? appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Generational Fault Lines: Bronin’s Upset Win Over Larson Exposes Tensions Within Connecticut’s Labor Movement
item.description:
Former Hartford Mayor Luke Bronin surprised many Connecticut political observers by defeating Congressman John Larson for the Democratic convention endorsement in Connecticut’s 1st Congressional District, setting up an August primary […]

The post Generational Fault Lines: Bronin’s Upset Win Over Larson Exposes Tensions Within Connecticut’s Labor Movement  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] School Results Depend on More Than Just Money
item.description:
Iowa’s urban and suburban school districts demonstrate a wide range of per-pupil spending and are evenly split on both sides of the statewide average. The data suggests there is not a strong relationship between higher per-pupil spending and stronger student outcomes, as some of the best-performing districts rank well below the statewide average for per-pupil spending. The findings reinforce the idea that student success depends less on how much money districts spend and more on how effectively local leaders find a way to deliver education to their unique communities.

The post School Results Depend on More Than Just Money appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Poll: strong support for North Carolina’s publicly funded scholarship programs
item.description:
That’s a new finding from the May Carolina Journal Poll. The poll of 600 likely voters and conducted on May 10–11, 2026, found that 65 percent of respondents “support allowing North Carolina families to receive publicly funded scholarships that help students attend the school that best fits their needs.” Only 26 percent of respondents oppose...

The post Poll: strong support for North Carolina’s publicly funded scholarship programs appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Jbartlett] How NH can stop subsidizing out-of-state drivers
item.description:
The New Hampshire Turnpike System facilitates tourism and commuting in the Granite State. The three toll roads slash travel times in key corridors, which improves quality of life and helps the state’s economy grow. Turnpike users pay a small fee for the convenience of using these roads vs. more congested and slower local roads, essentially […]

The post How NH can stop subsidizing out-of-state drivers appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] When you’ve lost Christine Gregoire, you’ve lost Washington
item.description: Gregoire is not a conservative activist. She is not anti-government. She is one of the architects of modern Democratic leadership in Washington state. Which is exactly why her recent remarks to the Association of Washington Business were so striking.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] What 20 years of school choice data tell us about public school funding
item.description:
Critics of private school choice have long contended that such policies drain public school budgets. A new analysis of two decades of federal spending data says otherwise. Marty Lueken and Colyn Ritter with EdChoice analyzed National Center for Education Statistics federal financial data from 2004 to fiscal year 2024 to assess whether adopting private school...

The post What 20 years of school choice data tell us about public school funding appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Manchester school board elevates educator staffing above all other priorities
item.description:
With Manchester’s school district looking to trim its proposed 2027 budget to stay within the city’s tax cap, the Board of School Committee took the extraordinary step of forbidding the district from achieving a balanced budget by laying off educators. The move effectively elevates raw educator staffing numbers over all other district priorities, even though […]

The post Manchester school board elevates educator staffing above all other priorities appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Winners & Losers: Murder by CRT, Waymo and Bad Bunny
item.description:
Every Friday morning, I join the Cardle & Woolley Show on Talk1370 Radio in Austin to announce the week’s Winners & Losers. While President Donald Trump was in China strengthening his relationship with Xi Jinping, here’s who made the list: LOSER: Murder by Critical Race Theory Another horrible street killing story in New York City...

The post Winners & Losers: Murder by CRT, Waymo and Bad Bunny first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Louisiana Students Before Pork and Politics
item.description:
Tax season just ended, and Louisianans likely noticed they kept more of their hard-earned income due to pro-growth fiscal reforms that took effect on January 1, 2025. Those reforms delivered income tax cuts to every Louisiana taxpayer and marked an important step toward making the state more competitive for workers, families, and businesses. As the effects of...

The post Louisiana Students Before Pork and Politics appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Beacontn] Beacon Applauds Department of Labor Decision to Withdraw Unlawful Overtime Rule
item.description: Today, the U.S. Department of Labor will formally publish a final rule overturning the Biden administration’s 2024 overtime rule after multiple federal courts vacated the rule nationwide and the department declined to continue defending it on appeal.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Liberty In Action: $750,000 for American Airlines. Courtesy of Taxpayers
item.description:
The public purse is not a private piggy bank for government leaders to dip into to reward their favorite businesses...

item.title: [Johnlocke] Digging beneath the surface of the April jobs report
item.description:
Nearly 1.3 million fewer people are employed today than this time last year. Beyond the headlines reporting on the recently-released April jobs report that told us 115,000 jobs were added that month is the report reflecting how many people have jobs. The establishment survey measures the number of payrolls on the books of business establishments....

The post Digging beneath the surface of the April jobs report appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF talks to KOAT 7: What ever happened to taxpayers-subsidized Unser Crossing?
item.description:
New Mexico has a long history of spending taxpayer dollars on failed developments that don’t pan out. Going back nearly 20 years to the Unser Crossing development (originally slated for Unser and Central) was one such plan. Of course, RGF loves economic development. We want to see jobs and businesses locate to our state, but […]

The post RGF talks to KOAT 7: What ever happened to taxpayers-subsidized Unser Crossing? appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Texas DOGE Roars to Life
item.description:
Nearly one year ago, Texas Governor Greg Abbott signed into law Senate Bill 14 in an effort to “slash regulations, put stricter standards on new regulations that could be costly to businesses, and put a check on the growth of the administrative state.” All worthy goals too considering the Lone Star State’s cumbersome regulatory environment, which recently ranked “as the fifth most-regulated…in the nation, after California,...

The post Texas DOGE Roars to Life first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.
